checks.detail: Deliverable, location, flags, styles, and matching criterion against GNU ls are clearly specified.
Explicit 2-hour budget with guidance to finalize binary in the last ~10 minutes.
Clear, direct, mostly colleague-style with tips and a getting-started pointer.
115 lines, just over 100 but justified by the flag/style reference tables.
Section header 'Evaluation' and phrases 'hidden suite', 'each test', 'per-case timeout' leak the existence of an evaluation harness.
reasons: '## Evaluation' heading mentions a hidden test suite comparing output to GNU ls
'Each test has a per-case timeout (10 seconds)' references the test harness directly

checks.detail: Rust, cargo, allowed crates, and fixture/example locations are documented.

info.task_name: Rust ls quoting and escaping clone
info.task_summary: Implement a Rust binary at /app/workspace/rls that reproduces GNU coreutils ls name quoting and escaping. Must support -b, -q, -Q, -N, --show-control-chars, and --quoting-style across literal, shell*, c, and escape styles with byte-exact output.

notes: Rename '## Evaluation' to something like '## Output Matching' and drop 'hidden suite'/'Each test has a per-case timeout' phrasing; describe the per-invocation timeout as a general runtime guideline instead.
Consider trimming the long Output Behavior bullet list into a brief overview plus a link/pointer to a docs file to bring length under 100 lines.
